root_text.sn35.114:1.1: “Santi, bhikkhave, cakkhuviññeyyā rūpā, iṭṭhā kantā manāpā piyarūpā kāmūpasaṁhitā rajanīyā.
root_text.sn35.114:1.2: Tañce bhikkhu abhinandati abhivadati ajjhosāya tiṭṭhati—
root_text.sn35.114:1.3: ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, bhikkhu āvāsagato mārassa, mārassa vasaṁ gato, paṭimukkassa mārapāso.
root_text.sn35.114:2.1: Santi, bhikkhave, jivhāviññeyyā rasā, iṭṭhā kantā manāpā piyarūpā kāmūpasaṁhitā rajanīyā.
root_text.sn35.114:2.2: Tañce bhikkhu abhinandati abhivadati ajjhosāya tiṭṭhati—
root_text.sn35.114:2.3: ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, bhikkhu āvāsagato mārassa, mārassa vasaṁ gato, paṭimukkassa mārapāso. Baddho so mārabandhanena …pe….
root_text.sn35.114:3.1: Santi, bhikkhave, manoviññeyyā dhammā, iṭṭhā kantā manāpā piyarūpā kāmūpasaṁhitā rajanīyā.
root_text.sn35.114:3.2: Tañce bhikkhu abhinandati abhivadati ajjhosāya tiṭṭhati—
root_text.sn35.114:3.3: ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, bhikkhu āvāsagato mārassa, mārassa vasaṁ gato, paṭimukkassa mārapāso.
root_text.sn35.114:4.1: Santi ca kho, bhikkhave, cakkhuviññeyyā rūpā, iṭṭhā kantā manāpā piyarūpā kāmūpasaṁhitā rajanīyā.
root_text.sn35.114:4.2: Tañce bhikkhu nābhinandati nābhivadati nājjhosāya tiṭṭhati—
root_text.sn35.114:4.3: ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, bhikkhu nāvāsagato mārassa, na mārassa vasaṁ gato, ummukkassa mārapāso.
root_text.sn35.114:4.4: Mutto so mārabandhanena na yathākāmakaraṇīyo pāpimato …pe….
root_text.sn35.114:5.1: Santi, bhikkhave, jivhāviññeyyā rasā, iṭṭhā kantā manāpā piyarūpā kāmūpasaṁhitā rajanīyā.
root_text.sn35.114:5.2: Tañce bhikkhu nābhinandati nābhivadati nājjhosāya tiṭṭhati—
root_text.sn35.114:5.3: ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, bhikkhu nāvāsagato mārassa, na mārassa vasaṁ gato, ummukkassa mārapāso. Mutto so mārabandhanena na yathākāmakaraṇīyo pāpimato …pe….
root_text.sn35.114:6.1: Santi, bhikkhave, manoviññeyyā dhammā, iṭṭhā kantā manāpā piyarūpā kāmūpasaṁhitā rajanīyā.
root_text.sn35.114:6.2: Tañce bhikkhu nābhinandati nābhivadati nājjhosāya tiṭṭhati—
root_text.sn35.114:6.3: ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, bhikkhu nāvāsagato mārassa, na mārassa vasaṁ gato, ummukkassa mārapāso.
root_text.sn35.114:6.4: Mutto so mārabandhanena na yathākāmakaraṇīyo pāpimato”ti.

translation_text.sn35.114:0.2: 12. The World and the Kinds of Sensual Stimulation
translation_text.sn35.114:1.1: “Mendicants, there are sights known by the eye, which are likable, desirable, agreeable, pleasant, sensual, and arousing.
translation_text.sn35.114:1.2: If a mendicant approves, welcomes, and keeps clinging to them,
translation_text.sn35.114:1.3: they’re called a mendicant trapped in Māra’s lair, fallen under Māra’s sway, and caught in Māra’s snare.
translation_text.sn35.114:1.4: They’re bound by Māra’s bonds, and the Wicked One can do with them what he wants.
translation_text.sn35.114:2.1: There are sounds … smells … tastes … touches …
translation_text.sn35.114:3.1: There are ideas known by the mind, which are likable, desirable, agreeable, pleasant, sensual, and arousing.
translation_text.sn35.114:3.2: If a mendicant approves, welcomes, and keep clinging to them,
translation_text.sn35.114:3.3: they’re called a mendicant trapped in Māra’s lair, fallen under Māra’s sway, and caught in Māra’s snare.
translation_text.sn35.114:3.4: They’re bound by Māra, and the Wicked One can do with them what he wants.
translation_text.sn35.114:4.1: There are sights known by the eye, which are likable, desirable, agreeable, pleasant, sensual, and arousing.
translation_text.sn35.114:4.2: If a mendicant doesn’t approve, welcome, and keep clinging to them,
translation_text.sn35.114:4.3: they’re called a mendicant not trapped in Māra’s lair, not fallen under Māra’s sway, and released from Māra’s snare.
translation_text.sn35.114:4.4: They’re free from Māra’s bonds, and the Wicked One cannot do with them what he wants.
translation_text.sn35.114:5.1: There are sounds … smells … tastes … touches …
translation_text.sn35.114:6.1: There are ideas known by the mind, which are likable, desirable, agreeable, pleasant, sensual, and arousing.
translation_text.sn35.114:6.2: If a mendicant doesn’t approve, welcome, and keep clinging to them,
translation_text.sn35.114:6.3: they’re called a mendicant not trapped in Māra’s lair, not fallen under Māra’s sway, and released from Māra’s snare.
translation_text.sn35.114:6.4: They’re free from Māra’s bonds, and the Wicked One cannot do with them what he wants.”
